title: Tantra
author: Osho
blurb: Osho's been a super controvesial philospher, but the reason why this is a good read is because it breaks a lot of social norms that we've made up in our head. Also a great read if you're an overthinker or live inside your head a lot like me.

title: Think like a monk
author: Jay Shetty
blurb: This book helped me quite literally think like a monk. Don't get too attached to your thoughts, live for a higher purpose besides yourself. Follow your Dharma.

title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
author: Eric Jorgenson
blurb: A great mental model to learn. That life is just a test, and the only way to win in life is if you get what you want out of it. Unlike other books, Naval goes more into depth about how leverage is the biggest key to building wealth. Naval also goes more into philosphy and like osho helps you break down a lot of social norms and helped me get over the fear of judgement.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
blurb: The best busisness book i've ever read. How to build monopolies, and exterminate your competition. Learnt the importance of creating companies that go from 0-1 rather than 1-n, because 0-1 is innovation, 1-n is just distribution. Innovation is always awarded and valued more than repurposing an innovated product for distribution.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
blurb: Learnt the importance of following your journey, regardless of the obstacles you may face. Everyone has their own treasure that they must find, and they have to overcome all the obstacles. You have to learn to trust in yourself and your heart. You may be scared, but instead of letting the emotion win, you must comfort your own emotions and use yourself as a tool to get yourself to your treasure.
